dialogue.utterance: Units 93 and 16 for one adult with breathing difficulty at the Richmond apartment complex.
Hello sir, I'm Alex, EMT with Emergency One Rescue. What made you call 911 today?
Well, I'm having trouble breathing, started about an hour ago.
Can you tell me your name and what today's date is?
It's Walter James. Today is June the second.
Could you tell me the name of this city?
We are in Richmond.
Airway is patent and open.
Respiratory rate is 19, even, and appropriate depth. He is breathing full sentences without difficulty.
Pulse is strong at 131, regular. Blood pressure is 153 over 90. Skin is warm and dry.
Can you describe where you feel the shortness of breath and if anything makes it worse?
I just feel gasping for air regardless of what I do. It's in my chest, mostly.

dialogue.micro_intent: S.A.M.P.L.E. → events leading up to the event
dialogue.utterance: Have you used any alcohol recently or in the past few hours?

dialogue.micro_intent: S.A.M.P.L.E. → events leading up to the event
dialogue.utterance: Yeah, I had a small amount of liquor about an hour ago.
Walter, I want to cover your medications and recent history. What prescriptions do you take?
I use Albuterol for COPD.
How long have you been managing COPD?
A couple of years-well-controlled unless there's an exacerbation.
On a scale of 0 to 10, how severe is the shortness of breath you're experiencing right now?
It's a 5. Not unbearable, but definitely noticeable.
Skin is dry, warm, and shows no signs of rashes, bruising, or cuts.
Pupils are equal, round, and reactive. No abnormalities found with the neck structure.
Walter, I'm going to listen to your lungs. Ready?
Please go ahead.
Pulse is 131, strong and regular.
Respiratory rate is 19, normal depth, and even rhythm.
Blood pressure is 153 over 90.
Oxygen saturation is 93 percent.
Glucose testing is not available, but not clinically indicated at this time.
Let's apply a cardiac monitor for a baseline reading. Can you confirm?
Patient will receive 2 LPM oxygen. Monitor is powering up.
Cardiac monitor is operational. Starting with 4-lead assessment.
4-lead monitoring is in place. No immediate cardiac issues noted.
By vital signs and EKG, we're following the COPD protocol and cardiac monitoring per protocol 2-7.
Pulse now is 118, still tachycardic.
Respiratory rate is steady at 15.
Blood pressure is 133 over 84.
Oxygen saturation has improved to 98 percent.
EKG now shows sinus tachycardia and signs of right atrial abnormality.
Your shortness of breath-is it better, worse, or the same?
It's about the same.
Continuous 2 LPM oxygen and cardiac monitoring are ongoing. Your symptoms are stable.
Tell me again about the events leading up to this-was there anything else significant?

dialogue.micro_intent: S.A.M.P.L.E. → events leading up to the event
dialogue.utterance: Nope, just that liquor hour before onset.

dialogue.micro_intent: S.A.M.P.L.E. → events leading up to the event
dialogue.utterance: And did you feel any pain or discomfort along with the breathing issues?
No, just the shortness of breath. No chest or abdominal pain.
Walter, can you tell me your name, date, city, and reason for calling 911?
I'm Walter James, June 2nd in Richmond. I had trouble breathing.
Walter, we will transport you to McGuire VA ER for further evaluation. Any issues with that?
Not at all. I'm satisfied with the hospital.
Walter is secured on the stretcher in Semi Fowler's. Five straps locked. Ready to go.
Last check before transport. Pulse 118-strong at radial. O2 at 98. Respiratory rate good.
Walter is en route with 2 LPM oxygen on a nasal cannula at 98% saturation and normal breathing.
He has COPD with possible exacerbation and alcohol intake in the last hour.
12-lead EKG shows right atrial enlargement, possible left atrial enlargement, and sinus tach. No acute MI changes.
Per protocol 2-7, we followed the standard COPD and cardiac monitoring procedures.
Walter is alert and cooperative. Currently condition and vitals are stable.
Walter is calm and oriented. No pain reported. Documents and vitals are all ready for the hospital team.
He has been placed into Room 21 at McGuire VA. No complications-care is fully passed to the ER nurses.
